title: When Assessing Outcomes: - Pause: Ask Yourself,
subtitle: Look Beyond Morals
description:
When assessing outcomes:
- Pause: Ask yourself, "Could societal factors have contributed here?"
- Contextualize: Consider external influences like environment, resources, or circumstances.
- Avoid blame: Shift from moral judgment to understanding.

Example: If someone misses a deadline, think about workload or system issues rather than assuming laziness.

howTo.title: Define the metric baseline
howTo.summary: Estimate how often you would have assigned blame in the past two weeks. This will serve as your baseline for measuring the effectiveness of the pause practice.

howTo.title: Apply the pause practice
howTo.summary: For each relevant event, take 7–10 minutes to pause, list situational possibilities, rate their plausibility, and decide on one immediate action to address the situation.

howTo.title: Log your outcomes
howTo.summary: After applying the pause practice, record the number of pauses taken and the minutes spent on evidence collection. This helps track your progress and effectiveness.

howTo.title: Review and adapt your approach
howTo.summary: After a week, evaluate your logged outcomes against your baseline. Identify any patterns and adjust your approach to improve your responses in future situations.

howTo.title: Check in with LifeOS Brali app
howTo.summary: Log your results and reflections in the Brali LifeOS app to track your progress and reinforce the habit. Access the app at https://metalhatscats.com/life-os/moral-judgment-bias-checker/.
